title: nepeta ucranica subsp. kopetdaghensis
intro: Nepeta ucranica subsp. kopetdaghensis, also known as Kopetdag catmint, is a subspecies of perennial herbaceous plant that belongs to the mint family. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, valued for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties, which help to relieve respiratory issues, such as coughs and colds. The plant's medicinal properties are attributed to its essential oils, particularly nepetalactone, which have been shown to exhibit antibacterial and antiviral activities. In herbal preparations, Nepeta ucranica subsp. kopetdaghensis is often used as a tea, infusion, or tincture to support respiratory health and alleviate symptoms of bronchitis, asthma, and other respiratory conditions.
